name: Uppsala
summary: Uppsala is an extremely safe university city with very low crime rates and excellent infrastructure. The biggest safety concerns are minor pickpocketing in crowded areas and occasional gang-related incidents in certain suburban areas.
quickVerdict: Uppsala is one of Sweden's safest cities, offering an exceptionally secure environment for tourists. With excellent public transportation, world-class healthcare, and a strong academic atmosphere, visitors can explore with confidence. The primary concerns are minor petty crimes like pickpocketing at the central station and tourist areas, plus the need to be aware of Sweden's current terrorism advisory level. The city's well-developed infrastructure, progressive values, and low crime rates make it ideal for all types of travelers, including solo females.

neighborhoods.name: Uppsala City Center
neighborhoods.description: The historic city center around Uppsala Cathedral and University is very safe with heavy foot traffic, good lighting, and regular police presence. Tourist-friendly with minimal crime concerns.

neighborhoods.name: Gamla Uppsala
neighborhoods.description: The ancient burial grounds and museum area is peaceful and safe during daylight hours. Less crowded in evenings but generally secure for visitors.

neighborhoods.name: Uppsala Castle Area
neighborhoods.description: Well-maintained tourist area with excellent safety standards. Popular with visitors and locals alike with minimal security concerns.

neighborhoods.name: Svartbäcken
neighborhoods.description: Mixed residential area that's generally safe but can have some disturbances. Exercise normal caution, especially during evening hours.

neighborhoods.name: Gottsunda
neighborhoods.description: Suburban area with higher crime rates and occasional gang-related activity. Listed as a particularly vulnerable area by Swedish police since 2017. Avoid unnecessary visits, especially at night.

neighborhoods.name: Gränby
neighborhoods.description: Outer residential area with some social challenges. Generally safe during daytime but exercise increased caution in evening hours.

scams.name: Fake Charity Collections
scams.description: People approach tourists claiming to collect donations for local causes or charities, pressuring for immediate cash donations.
scams.howToAvoid: Only donate through official channels or recognized organizations. Ask for proper identification and decline on-the-spot requests.

scams.name: Overpriced Taxi Rides
scams.description: Unscrupulous taxi drivers may take longer routes, charge higher fares than the meter shows, or claim the meter shows euros instead of Swedish krona.
scams.howToAvoid: Use reputable taxi companies, confirm the meter is running, check the fare sticker on the window, and use rideshare apps when possible.

scams.name: Pickpocketing at Central Station
scams.description: Thieves target distracted tourists at Uppsala Central Station and during festivals, often working in groups with distraction techniques.
scams.howToAvoid: Keep valuables in front pockets or hidden pouches, stay alert in crowded areas, and avoid displaying expensive items openly.

scams.name: ATM Card Skimming
scams.description: Criminals may approach at ATMs offering to help avoid fees while attempting to scan cards or observe PIN numbers.
scams.howToAvoid: Use ATMs inside banks or well-lit areas, cover your PIN when entering, and politely decline help from strangers at ATMs.

soloFemale.overview: Uppsala is exceptionally safe for solo female travelers, consistently ranking among Europe's safest cities for women. Sweden's progressive gender equality values create a welcoming environment where solo female travelers face minimal harassment or safety concerns. The university atmosphere adds to the safe, intellectual environment.
tips: Walk confidently at night in well-lit central areas
Trust your instincts if something feels off
Use reliable transportation options late at night rather than walking alone in suburban areas

nightSafety.overview: Uppsala's nightlife is generally safe with good lighting and regular police presence in central areas. About 60% of residents and visitors feel fully secure during nighttime hours. The student population creates a vibrant but respectful night scene with multiple safety options available.
tips: Stick to well-lit, populated areas in the city center
Use official taxis or rideshare apps for late-night transportation
Keep emergency numbers saved in your phone and inform others of your plans

transport.metro: Uppsala doesn't have a metro system but has excellent regional train connections to Stockholm and other cities via Swedish Rail.
transport.rideshare: Uber and Bolt operate in Uppsala providing safe, tracked transportation options. Both apps work well and offer competitive pricing compared to traditional taxis.
transport.taxis: Licensed taxis are safe but expensive. Always use companies with yellow license plates and confirm the fare sticker on the window showing rates for a 10km journey.
transport.tips: Public buses operated by UL are extremely safe, clean, and reliable. Download the UL app for easy ticket purchases and real-time updates.

customs: Punctuality is essential - arrive exactly on time, not early or late
Personal space is highly valued - maintain respectful distance in conversations
Fika (coffee breaks) are important social rituals for building relationships
Environmental consciousness is expected - recycle properly and respect nature
Direct, honest communication is preferred over small talk or indirect approaches

health.overview: Uppsala has world-class healthcare with Uppsala University Hospital (Akademiska Sjukhuset) being one of Sweden's leading medical facilities. The Swedish healthcare system offers universal coverage with excellent emergency services.
health.water: Tap water is completely safe to drink and among the cleanest in the world. Bring a reusable bottle and fill up anywhere.
health.vaccinations: No special vaccinations required. Routine vaccinations should be up to date including MMR, DPT, flu, and COVID-19 as recommended.
health.altitude: Uppsala is at sea level with a temperate climate. Winter temperatures can reach -10°C (14°F) so pack appropriate clothing for the season.

emergency.usEmbassy: U.S. Embassy Stockholm: +46 (8) 783-5300, Dag Hammarskjölds Väg 31, SE-115 89 Stockholm. For emergencies after hours: +46 (8) 783-5300

faq.a: Yes, Uppsala is extremely safe for tourists with very low crime rates and excellent infrastructure. It's consistently ranked among Sweden's safest cities.
Uppsala is generally safe at night, especially in the well-lit city center. About 60% of people feel fully secure during nighttime hours, though normal urban precautions are recommended.

faq.q: Is Uppsala safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Absolutely. Uppsala is one of Europe's safest cities for solo female travelers, ranking #6 in Sweden with a 4.4/5 safety rating. Sweden's strong gender equality values create a very welcoming environment.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Uppsala?
faq.a: Avoid Gottsunda, especially at night, as it's been designated a vulnerable area by Swedish police since 2017. Gränby and some parts of Svartbäcken require increased caution during evening hours.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Uppsala?
faq.a: Yes, public transport in Uppsala is extremely safe, clean, and reliable. Buses are well-maintained with good lighting and regular schedules. The UL transit system is considered one of the safest in Europe.
